text:
<think>I should start by explaining the basic genetic relationship between an individual and their parents.</think>
A person inherits half of their genetic material from their mother and half from their father. 
<think>Now I should address the genetic relationship between siblings, considering they have the same parents but independent assortment can change the outcome.</think>
Full siblings also share about half of their genetic material on average, due to inheriting different combinations of their parents' genes.
<think>I should clarify that, on average, the proportion of shared genes is roughly equal between siblings and between parent and child.</think>
Therefore, a person shares approximately 50% of their genes with each parent and with each full sibling.
<think>It's helpful to note that the exact percentage can vary slightly due to the random nature of genetic inheritance, but the average is the same.</think>
The actual percentage can vary a little between siblings, but the average remains about 50% in both cases.
